opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Every time I walk to the letterbox, my calves cramp up so painfully I have to stop and rest for five minutes before I can move.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Calf cramping on walking short distances has worsened over the past week, restricting mobility and causing distress.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradually over about 6 months, worse in the last week
symptom_narrative.duration: 6 months of exertional calf cramp, acutely worse over the past week
symptom_narrative.character: a cramping, tight calf pain that comes on with walking
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: painful enough that he has to stop and rest for 5-10 minutes, and he is fearful of leaving the house
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both calves, coming on when walking
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: predictably after walking about 50 metres, fully relieved by 5-10 minutes of rest, then recurs on walking again
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: stopping and resting completely relieves it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: nothing else - no chest or neurological symptoms; denies constant rest pain, ulcers, or sudden numbness/coldness in the legs
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: this has been building over 6 months but never this limiting
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: mobility heavily restricted; fearful of leaving the house; independent living feels threatened

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; largely sedentary; has avoided seeing doctors for years
social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: sedentary lifestyle with a highly processed diet

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: has avoided doctors for years and is worried about losing his independence
